event_name: National Tom Sawyer Days - Kiwanis Arts & Crafts Festival
address: Downtown Hannibal, MO (Central Park area, 320 S 3rd St)
audience: General public including seniors; major multi-day community festival with broad age range
tabling_details: Outdoor arts and crafts vendor booths in block-square park downtown. Booth space $150 + $14 city merchandisers license. Food vendor spaces $500 each (includes electricity). All booths must be paid in full by June 15, 2026. Contact Kiwanis Club of Hannibal.
cost: Booth space: $150 + $14 license for arts/crafts; $500 for food vendors
notes: 71st Annual National Tom Sawyer Days runs June 27-July 5 2026. Arts & Crafts Festival runs concurrently in downtown Hannibal. Organized by Hannibal Evening Kiwanis. Festival includes fence painting frog jumping fireworks carnival live music parade arts & crafts show. Seniors are a regular part of the community attendance. Window start is June 28 so most of the event falls within scope.

venue: Princeton Square (east side in front of Mercer County Senior Center)
address: 110 N Broadway area, Princeton Square, Princeton, MO 64673
audience: All ages; held directly in front of and in partnership with the Mercer County Senior Center, attracting significant senior attendance; Senior Center expressed interest in collaborating on summer ice cream social
tabling_details: FREE vendor spaces. Vendors set up on the east side of the Princeton Square in front of the Senior Center, Saturdays 8am-noon. No charge to vendors. Activities planned for each Saturday. May pause during Mercer County Fair week (July 15-19). Contact Princeton Chamber at 660-748-4009.
cost: Free
notes: Began May 2, 2026; runs Saturdays 8am-noon through summer (end date not specified publicly but at least through fall). Confirmed by kttn.com and northwestmoinfo.com. Date range shown here is remainder of search window (June 28 - Sept 26, 2026). Note: scheduling conflict noted with Mercer County Fair (July 15-19) so may skip those Saturdays. Senior Center partnership confirmed. Full calendar on Facebook page 'Princeton Community Market and Super Saturdays.'

venue: North Elson and East Washington Streets
address: N. Elson St. & E. Washington St.
audience: General community shoppers; farmers markets consistently attract 55+ shoppers seeking fresh produce and local goods
tabling_details: Vendor spots available through the Kiwanis Club of Kirksville; market operates every Saturday May through October 7am-12pm. Vendor applications through market management.
notes: Recurring weekly market every Saturday May through October, 7:00am-12:00pm. Dates shown cover the search window (June 28 - September 26, 2026). Saturdays during this window: July 4, 11, 18, 25; Aug 1, 8, 15, 22, 29; Sept 5, 12, 19, 26. Operated by Kirksville Kiwanis Club. Also referenced at Facebook page kirksvillekiwanisfarmersmarket.

address: 120 W. 1st Street (corner of W 1st St and N Market St), Milan, MO 63556
audience: General public including seniors; farmers market draws older adult shoppers in small rural communities
tabling_details: Weekly outdoor farmers market with multiple vendors selling produce and goods. Vendor spots available; contact Sullivan County Health Department at (660) 265-4141 for vendor inquiries. Market runs Thursdays 2:30-6pm (also reported as 3pm-6pm) May through October.
cost: Free admission
notes: Weekly recurring market, Thursdays, May through October. Dates within the search window (2026-06-28 to 2026-09-26) include Thursdays from July 2 through September 24, 2026. Operated by Sullivan County Health Department. Sells Missouri produce: peaches, blackberries, tomatoes, potatoes, zucchini, cantaloupes, watermelon, onions, etc. Date listed is first Thursday in window; end_date is last Thursday in window.

venue: East John Street near Twin Parks
audience: General community; seniors are a significant portion of shoppers at small-town Missouri farmers markets
tabling_details: Open vendor market — vendors must have homegrown or homemade items and be set up by 3:00 PM. Market also lists a Saturday market at Prenger's Foods parking lot (542 S Main St) year-round 8am-noon.
notes: Operates Thursdays 3-6 PM, May through October (Saturdays year-round 8am-noon). Also referenced by Linn County Leader as 'open every Thursday.' Market is at E. John Street near Twin Parks in Brookfield MO 64628. Recurring weekly market; individual Thursday dates within the search window (June 28 - Sept 26, 2026) are every Thursday from July through September 24. No explicit senior-focused programming found, but rural small-town farmers markets in MO consistently draw 65+ shoppers.

event_name: Princeton July 4th Independence Day Celebration (Junktique & More on the Square)
venue: Princeton Square and Hal England Community Room
audience: General community including seniors; all-day street festival on the town square with vendors, food trucks, craft show, live music, and fireworks
tabling_details: Vendors invited to set up on the Princeton Square (Junktique and More). Cost: $25 if no electricity needed. Craft show held at Hal England Community Room. Food trucks also expected. Chamber of Commerce organizes; contact at 660-748-4009.
cost: $25 (no electricity needed)
notes: Confirmed by kttn.com articles: July 4 celebration with Junktique vendors on the square ($25/booth, no electricity), craft show at Hal England Community Room, food trucks, live band (Slippery When Wet tribute at 8pm), fireworks by Glenn Kirby at dusk. America's 250th birthday celebration. Source also: https://www.northwestmoinfo.com/local-news/princeton-chamber-discusses-4th-of-july-celebration-plans/

event_name: Kirksville Annual Red White and Blue Festival
audience: General public/community; civic celebration attracting all ages including seniors
tabling_details: Kirksville Arts Association manages vendor/exhibitor applications for festival events in Kirksville; pie contests, car show, parade, BBQ, and band concerts draw community-wide attendance including seniors
notes: July 3: United Way Pie Sale and Auction (4-7pm), MSK Red White & BBQ (5-7pm), Kirksville Community Band Concert and Summer on the Square at Courthouse lawn. July 4: Pancake Breakfast (7-11am), All-American Car Show, Independence Parade, Fireworks. Vendor applications referenced via Kirksville Arts Association.

event_name: Hurdland Fourth of July Celebration (154th Annual)
audience: General community; multi-generational audience including seniors; features bingo, antique tractor show, classic car show, and antique/collectibles vendors that attract older adults
tabling_details: Event includes commercial/retail, crafts, antique/collectibles, and food vendor exhibitor categories. ~20 exhibitors expected. Contact organizers via hurdland4thofjulycelebration.org for booth applications.
cost: Free admission
notes: 154th consecutive annual celebration; oldest July 4th celebration in northeast Missouri; located on Hwy 6 between Kirksville (15 mi west) and Edina (7 mi east); features bingo, corn hole, classic car show, antique tractor show, live music, raffles, concessions. Festivalnet.com lists antique/collectibles, commercial/retail, corp./information, crafts, and food exhibitor categories. 2026 dates confirmed by festivalnet.com.

audience: General public including seniors; county fair format with commercial/exhibit buildings historically attracting all ages including 65+ community
tabling_details: County fairs of this type include commercial exhibit buildings for vendors; registration via FairEntry.com; out-of-county early registration closed June 15, 2026 but open until check-in
notes: Dates July 5-12, 2026 confirmed via multiple search results including FairEntry.com registration page and Macon Area Chamber of Commerce listing. Kids show animals and art projects; commercial exhibit building open during fair days.

audience: General public including families and seniors; county fair draws broad 65+ rural attendance
tabling_details: County fairs standardly include commercial vendor and exhibitor booths. Vendor inquiries can be directed to the Scotland County Fair Board via scotlandcountyfair.com/contact-us/. Board contacts include Lane Pence (President) and Alexis Kittle (Secretary).
cost: Free admission first three days (July 6-8); $20 daily admission July 9-11 starting at 2pm; season passes $55
notes: 2026 theme is 'Homegrown Happiness.' Features entertainment, food, rides, 4-H and FFA livestock shows and projects, and a carnival (PBJ Happee Days Shows) running July 9-13. Date range from Memphis Democrat search snippet (July 6-14); scotlandcountyfair.com event listing shows July 4-11 — exact end date may differ slightly. Verify directly with fair board.

audience: All ages, broad community attendance including seniors/65+ population in Sullivan County
tabling_details: County fair with food concessions and commercial/vendor exhibitor opportunities. Fair has a dedicated Fair Vendors section. Proceeds from concessions benefit Sullivan County 4-H. Commercial vendors and exhibitors typically participate at county fairs of this size.
notes: Confirmed July 6-12, 2026 by two separate KTTN (local north Missouri radio/news) articles. Activities include Fair Queen Pageant (July 7, Milan Community Center), dog show, 4-H judging, livestock shows (rabbit, poultry, goat, sheep, swine, beef, horse), free Farm Bureau breakfast July 11, and Market Animal Premium Sale. Food concessions run throughout the fair. The Sullivan County Exposition Center & Fairgrounds is a community hub for Sullivan County events.

audience: General public / families; county fairs in rural Missouri draw heavy 55+ attendance for livestock shows, pageants, and grandstand events
tabling_details: Vendor application page exists at lewiscountyfair.com; outdoor vendor setup documented (8 am–6 pm); food and commercial vendors accepted annually
notes: 49th annual fair confirmed for 2026. Exact 2026 dates not publicly confirmed in sources accessed — 2025 (48th annual) ran July 8–12; dates above are estimated based on that pattern. Verify exact 2026 dates at lewiscountyfair.wixsite.com or call 573-497-2770. Features 4-H/FFA livestock shows, carnival, grandstand events, parade, pageants, food vendors. Seniors (60+) historically attend in large numbers.

audience: General public including seniors/families; county fair attracts broad 55+ rural audience
tabling_details: County fairs routinely host commercial vendor booths; specific 2026 vendor application info not publicly confirmed online. Contact Ralls County Extension (573-985-7111) for vendor details.
notes: Dates July 9-13, 2026 confirmed by multiple search results. The 2025 FairEntry listing (fairentry.com/public/EnterFair/22396) also covers this fair. Located in Center, MO (Ralls County seat area). Historically a 4-H/livestock fair; commercial vendor opportunities are standard for county fairs but a specific 2026 vendor application was not found online.

audience: General public including seniors; SNAP/EBT accepted indicating broad community reach
tabling_details: 35+ vendors participate each market day; vendor applications accepted — call 660-263-6643 x 212 or email farmers@randolphcountyhealth.org
cost: Free to attend
notes: Held every other Thursday, 4–7 pm, from May 28 through October 1, 2026. Confirmed 2026 dates within the search window: July 9, July 23, Aug 6, Aug 20, Sep 3, Sep 17. Start date listed is first in-window date; end date is last in-window date. Accepts SNAP/EBT with Double Up Food Bucks matching.

audience: General community including families and seniors; 4-H/FFA youth exhibitors with broad public attendance
tabling_details: The fair has commercial vendor booths available via application through the fair organizers. Vendor applications accepted by fair vendor coordinator; contact linncountyyouthfair@gmail.com or (660) 895-5123.
notes: 2025 fair ran July 9-20; 2026 dates estimated based on prior year pattern — exact 2026 dates not confirmed on accessible pages as of research date. The fair is a 4-H/FFA youth fair but draws broad community attendance including seniors. Commercial vendor building closes at 9 p.m. nightly. Apply via Eventeny.

audience: Heritage/agricultural show that consistently draws older adult and senior audiences; retired farmers and rural community members are the primary demographic
tabling_details: Dedicated vendor contact: Darin Maupin at 660-833-7962. Vendors must contact Darin Maupin to secure a booth. Event has explicit vendor program.
notes: Annual show of antique equipment demonstrating old ways of rural life. Held at Paris City Fairgrounds, Paris, MO 65275. Mailing address: Mark Twain Old Threshers Association Inc. PO Box 14, Paris, MO 65275. Vendor contact Darin Maupin is also Vice President, address 415 E Locust, Paris, MO 65275. Heritage shows of this type draw significant 65+ attendance.

audience: General public, families, seniors; annual county fair with commercial vendor section
tabling_details: Fair historically includes commercial vendors/exhibitors section open to outside vendors
notes: Annual county youth fair held in early-to-mid July at the Salisbury fairgrounds. 2024 dates were July 7-12; 2026 dates of July 11-18 were cited in search result snippets but the discoverchariton.com events page listed 2026 dates as 'to be announced' as of the search date. Verify exact 2026 dates directly before tabling. Commercial vendors have participated in past years.

audience: General public, all ages including seniors; county fair draws broad community attendance
tabling_details: Commercial indoor vendor/exhibitor application available per NEMO Fair website; county fair format with commercial booths standard
notes: 5-day fair; opening day July 13 with free admission; runs July 14-18. Features livestock shows, carnival, FFA exhibits, truck and tractor pull, demolition derby, entertainment. Indoor vendor application referenced on fair website.

audience: General public including strong 65+ rural/agricultural community attendance; county fairs in small Missouri counties draw significant senior crowds
tabling_details: Fair has a Commercial Building with vendor/exhibitor spaces. County fairs typically offer commercial booth rental. Contact fair at (660) 327-4158 for vendor application details.
notes: Multiple sources confirm July 13-18, 2026 dates at Monroe County Fairgrounds in Paris, MO. Tradeshowz.com lists this as Monroe County Fair 2026 | Paris, MO. Facebook group post title confirms 'July 13-18, 2026*** Location: Monroe County Fairgrounds.' Fair website is monroecounty-fair.org. Has Commercial Building where vendor/exhibitor space is available.

audience: General public / all ages including significant senior/65+ attendance; county fair tradition draws older adults
tabling_details: Commercial vendor booths available in the Commercial Building (8ft x 10ft, $75 each; no food/beverage except pre-packaged). Outside food vendor spaces also available ($100). Vendors must purchase a season ticket or day pass. Applications available on the fair website.
cost: Admission fees apply for fairgoers; vendor booth fees: $75 (commercial/indoor), $100 (outside food vendor)
notes: Annual fair established 1882. Events confirmed on July 15 (baking contest, Commercial Building), July 16, and July 17 (IFPA Truck & Tractor Pull, Kid's Pedal Tractor Pull). Also features Demolition Derby, Supercross, Queen Contest, Talent Show, BBQ/Baking/Garden contests, carnival, and vendors. Vendor info page: clarkcountyfairkahoka.com/vendorinformation

audience: All ages; historically includes a dedicated Senior/Democrat Day with bingo, free meals, and vendor booths for seniors 60+
tabling_details: County fair with commercial exhibitor spaces; past Senior Day events have featured vendor booths and community partner tables (Human Service Council and others). Contact Mercer County Extension at 660-748-3315 for exhibitor/vendor applications.
notes: 2026 fair dates July 15-19 confirmed by multiple sources (tradeshowz.com, kttn.com, northwestmoinfo.com). Fair historically includes a Senior/Democrat Day on Thursday with bingo, door prizes, vendor booths, and free meals. Over 11,000 attend annually. Source also: https://tradeshowz.com/events/13120/mercer-county-fair and wrmj.com article on Senior Day.

audience: All ages; county fair draws broad community attendance including seniors/65+ crowd
tabling_details: County fair includes commercial vendor and exhibitor spaces; preregistration required through FairEntry.com for exhibitors selling at the fair. Fair features commercial vendors, arts & crafts, food, livestock, and youth exhibits.
notes: Dates reported as July 16-20 by some sources and July 18-22 by others; the July 18-22 range appeared in the most recent references and matches a Thursday-Monday pattern consistent with prior years. Family Day (Sunday) is a free public event. The 2026 fair book was published and applications were being accepted as of early 2026.

audience: All ages including heavy senior participation; traditional county fair with livestock, crafts, entertainment
tabling_details: Vendor/exhibitor applications available at randolphcountymofair.com; exhibitor agreement and fee form downloadable; online submission accepted
notes: Annual Randolph County 4-H and FFA Youth Fair. 2025 dates were July 18–23; 2026 dates of July 18–26 were cited in one search result but could not be independently verified via direct page fetch. Confirm exact 2026 dates at randolphcountymofair.com or by calling 660-269-9656.

audience: Antique, heritage, and collectible enthusiasts; audience strongly skews 55+/senior as the event celebrates late 19th and early 20th century machinery and collectibles
tabling_details: Vendor information page exists at macon-flywheel.com/vendor-information; event includes a large flea market with vendor spaces; contact macon.flywheel@gmail.com for vendor details
cost: $10 admission for all 4 days; kids 13 and under free
notes: July 23-26, 2026 dates confirmed via search results. 40-acre Macon County Park features antique steam engines, tractors, gas engines, machinery, cars, trucks, large flea market, live music, museum tours, food. Audience strongly skews older/senior due to antique/heritage focus. Official vendor information page on club website.

audience: General community including seniors/families; county fair with broad demographic attendance
tabling_details: Commercial vendor booths available; contact Committee Chair Christy Dempsey at 573-213-9876 for booth space. Fair features vendors, food, livestock, live music, rodeo, and more.
cost: Adult admission $20–$25 depending on day; vendor booth fees by application
notes: Annual county fair running July 28–August 1, 2026. Commercial vendor/booth space available by contacting Christy Dempsey. Fair includes vendors, food, 4-H/livestock, live music, truck/tractor pull, demolition derby, rodeo. Broad community event with significant senior attendance typical of county fairs.

address: Flower City Park, N Breckenridge Ave, Palmyra, MO 63461
audience: General public / seniors (Senior Night debut in 2026; senior discounts all week)
tabling_details: Vendor/exhibitor/concessionaire spaces available both outdoor and indoor. Contact office.mcfg@gmail.com for inquiries. Registration via FairEntry at marioncounty.fairentry.com.
cost: General admission tickets; BOGO deal for seniors on Senior Night ($8 for two tickets)
notes: Senior Night is making its debut at the Marion County Fair in 2026 with special senior discounts all week. Fair features livestock shows, horse shows, dog shows, arts and crafts exhibits, evening performances, rodeos, merchant vendors, senior citizens day, veterans day, kids days, rides, and fair food. Dates confirmed via multiple sources including allevents.in and Palmyra Chamber of Commerce.

audience: General public; Senior Night (new for 2026) specifically targets 65+ with BOGO ticket deal; seniors receive discount all week
tabling_details: Indoor air-conditioned Marketplace with 10x10 booths available for commercial vendors/exhibitors. Email office.mcfg@gmail.com for vendor applications. Senior Night debuts in 2026 with BOGO admission deal for seniors.
notes: Fair runs August 1-8, 2026 at Flower City Park, Palmyra, MO 63461 (Marion County, which borders Ralls County to the north). Senior Night is brand new for 2026 - exclusive BOGO deal (2 tickets for $8) on one evening during fair week. Commercial vendor Marketplace confirmed. Additional contacts: Denise Damron 573-795-6437, Dakota Damron 573-501-1493.

audience: General community including seniors; traditional small-town county fair draws all ages with heavy senior attendance
tabling_details: Search results confirm the fair includes vendors and activities throughout the multi-day event; contact fair organizers for booth applications
notes: Annual Higbee Fair in Randolph County includes carnival, musical entertainment, and vendors. Dates August 13–15, 2026 confirmed in search results. Higbee is a city in Randolph County, MO.

audience: All ages; large community festival draws seniors/65+ along with families and general public
tabling_details: Festival includes craft and retail vendor booths open 8 a.m.-4 p.m. on Saturday. Vendor applications accepted; general vendor and food vendor categories available. Contact Mexico Jaycees at mexicomojaycees@gmail.com for vendor application.
cost: Free admission for attendees; vendor booth fees apply
notes: Annual festival hosted by Mexico Jaycees. Includes parade, live music, car show, carnival, beer garden, cornhole tournament, craft and retail vendors, and food. August 20-22 dates reported by multiple sources for 2026 but noted as tentative/not officially confirmed on some sites; verify directly with organizers.

audience: General community; multi-generational; craft shows and community festivals in small rural towns draw a high proportion of seniors 65+
tabling_details: Event explicitly features Courtyard Vendors of all kinds and Food Vendors. Contact: 660-216-1065 or KCcornfest@gmail.com or via Facebook page Knox County Corn Festival - Official.
notes: Annual festival held the first weekend after Labor Day in Edina, MO. Features craft show, baby show, talent contest, car show, small kids rides, basketball tournament, daily entertainment, raffle prizes, and local group-sponsored meals. 2026 dates (Sept 4-6) confirmed by festivalnet.com and The Edina Sentinel. Vendor categories include courtyard vendors and food vendors.

audience: General public; artisan/craft/antique dealer festival with mixed-age audience; 55+ crowd well represented at artisan and antique-dealer events of this type
tabling_details: Mainstreet Macon Downtown Association actively recruits artisan, crafter, and specialty vendors; call 660-385-2811 for vendor registration; hosted by Macon Area Chamber of Commerce
notes: September 5, 2026 date confirmed via multiple search results. Event features high-end visual arts, quality crafters, antique dealers, live musical entertainment, food, and wine on historic downtown square. Vendor recruitment confirmed via centralmoinfo.com report on Mainstreet Macon Downtown Association call for vendors.

audience: General county fair audience; attracts broad community including large senior population; includes livestock shows, 4-H, demolition derby, carnival, commercial booths, baby show, entertainment concerts
tabling_details: Indoor commercial booths available in sizes from 10 ft to 30 ft wide. Open to local businesses, commercial goods, handcrafted wares, government agencies, political parties and candidates, and civic organizations. Priority given to returning vendors. Apply via putnamcountyfairunionvillemo.com.
cost: Daily entry $25 at gate; season pass $50 (through September 4) or $55 at gate; kids 12 and under free
notes: Annual county fair held first Tuesday–Saturday following Labor Day. For 2026, Labor Day is September 7, so the fair runs September 8–12, 2026. Confirmed 2026 entertainers include Rhonda Vincent (Sept 9) and Joe Nichols (Sept 12, 7:30 PM). Senior Citizens Day on Wednesday (Sept 9) with free admission for seniors 62+. Source also corroborated by KTTN radio (kttn.com) and Bandsintown/Spotify concert listings.

event_name: Putnam County Fair — Senior Citizens Day
audience: General fair audience with dedicated Senior Citizens Day; seniors 62+ admitted free; senior activities 9am–1pm in Entertainment Tent with music 10am–1pm
tabling_details: Indoor commercial booths available throughout the fair week (10–30 ft wide); booth types include local businesses, civic organizations, government agencies, and commercial vendors. Fair organizers note that commercial exhibitors are encouraged to staff their booths on Senior Citizens Day.
cost: Seniors 62+ free on Senior Citizens Day; general daily entry $25; season pass $50–$55
notes: Senior Citizens Day is held annually on Wednesday of fair week. The 2026 fair runs first Tuesday–Saturday after Labor Day (Sept 7, 2026), placing the fair September 8–12 and Senior Day on September 9. Rhonda Vincent is confirmed to perform September 9 at 7 PM; Joe Nichols performs Saturday September 12. Commercial booth vendor info at putnamcountyfairunionvillemo.com.

audience: General public including seniors; community festival with parade and craft and food and vendor booths
tabling_details: Festival historically features handmade goods, art, clothing, jewelry, direct sales vendors, food trucks; vendor/craft booth applications available through organizers
notes: Annual September festival held the second weekend of September in Keytesville MO (Chariton County). Second weekend of September 2026 falls on September 12-13. One search result snippet cited September 18-20 but that would be the third weekend; the established pattern is the second weekend. 2026 dates listed as 'to be announced' on discoverchariton.com. Confirm exact 2026 dates with organizers before planning tabling. Suitable senior audience as a community festival with craft and vendor booths.

audience: Community members including seniors; free health screenings and demonstrations and health resources attract older adults
tabling_details: Formal exhibitor registration process; each exhibitor provided 8-foot table with tablecloth and two chairs. 50+ local and national exhibitors participated in 2025 event. Contact kirksvilleexpo@atsu.edu to register as exhibitor.
cost: Free to attend
notes: Annual event hosted by A.T. Still University. 2025 event was September 6, 2025 (9am-3pm); 2026 date of September 12 identified in search results. Features free health screenings (dental, skin, oral cancer, hearing), food trucks, demonstrations, raffles, children's fun zone. Draws students, healthcare professionals, and community members. Strong senior attendance given free health screenings.

audience: Vintage/antique shoppers skewing 45+/senior; event draws ~15000 attendees
tabling_details: 100+ vendor booths; 2026 vendor application form available at form.jotform.com/253515967641162; contact Moberly Area Chamber of Commerce at chamber@moberlychamber.com
cost: Free admission
notes: One of the largest vintage markets in the Midwest, featuring vintage, antique, handmade, repurposed, and specialty items. Runs 9 am – 5 pm both days. Vendor application form confirmed for 2026 season. Hosted by Moberly Area Chamber of Commerce.

audience: General community including seniors; annual downtown street festival drawing broad community attendance
tabling_details: Craft and food vendor booths available for $35 registration fee through the Bowling Green MO Chamber of Commerce. Both seller (vendor) and non-seller (exhibitor) spots available, including civic organizations and businesses. Food vendors must comply with Pike County Health Department regulations and provide liability insurance.
cost: Free admission; vendor/exhibitor registration $35
contact_name: Bowling Green Missouri Chamber of Commerce
notes: Annual festival held on the second Saturday in September on the downtown square. Features local food and craft vendors, car/truck/tractor show (Bowling Green Lions Club), parade, live music, tours of Champ Clark's Honey Shuck. Free admission. Vendor registration fee $35 for merchandise, non-profits, civic orgs, businesses, artists, and food/beverage vendors. Event date September 12, 2026 confirmed as second Saturday in September.

audience: General public / all ages; craft barn, flea market, rodeo, and mule events strongly attract 55+/65+ crowd
tabling_details: Craft vendor booths available in a large craft barn; approximately 25 businesses display and sell items (quilts, rugs, wood crafts, metal art, baskets, ceramics, jewelry, homemade soap, etc.). Flea market space also available. Contact festival organizer for vendor applications.
cost: Vendor fees vary; contact organizer for details
notes: Annual festival held since 1986 on the 3rd full weekend of September. In 2025 it ran September 18-20 (Thu-Sun); 2026 dates estimated September 17-20. Features mule parade, mule shows, craft barn with ~25 vendors, flea market, rodeo, trail rides, music, and food. Craft barn open Fri-Sun from 8am. Confirmed vendor/craft booth opportunity. Source also at greatriverroad.com/art-events/clark-county-mule-festival

audience: General community including seniors; annual fall festival drawing crowds from across the region to Princeton and Calamity Jane's birthplace
tabling_details: Food and craft vendors confirmed as part of the event. West side of the square reserved for Community Market vendors; remainder of square open for garage sale booths and other vendors. Contact Princeton Chamber of Commerce at 660-748-4009 for vendor information.
notes: September 18-20, 2026 confirmed by multiple sources. Theme: '250 Years of Freedom.' Grand Marshalls: Shane and Marcia Cox. Features food and craft vendors, blacksmith and beekeeping demonstrations, pony rides, petting zoo, face painting, parade, car show (Sept 21 historically). Source also: https://www.kttn.com/calamity-jane-days-offers-a-variety-of-events-in-princeton-september-20-22/

address: Glascock's Landing, historic downtown Hannibal, MO
audience: General public; seniors attend; broad community event with arts/crafts, farmers market, classic car cruise, pumpkin carving
tabling_details: Event includes vendors, farmers market, arts and crafts. Falls festival benefits the Rotary Club of Hannibal. Vendor participation implied by event format.
cost: Free to attend (public festival)
notes: 11th Annual Harvest Hootenanny. Includes live music, bands, beer, food, vendors, petting zoo, bounce houses, pony rides, classic car cruise, farmers market, arts and crafts, pumpkin carving. Benefits the Rotary Club of Hannibal. Dates confirmed as September 18-19, 2026 via Hannibal Chamber and visithannibal.com search snippets.

event_name: Red Barn Arts and Crafts Festival
audience: General public including seniors; arts and crafts festivals historically draw strong 55+ attendance
tabling_details: Juried exhibitor application process; 100+ booths; applications for exhibitors and food vendors open online through Kirksville Arts Association. 52nd annual event in 2026.
cost: Free to attend
notes: 52nd annual Red Barn Arts and Crafts Festival organized by Kirksville Arts Association. Juried event with standards for design, originality, and execution. Attracts nearly 10,000 attendees to 100+ booths on the downtown Courthouse Square. September 19 date identified in 2026 Missouri Festival Guide and search results. Past events held on various September Saturdays.
